changeSet.author: travel-insurance
changeSet.comment: Pre-migration data quality check. Ensures all existing agreements reference valid medical levels and countries. Fails fast with a descriptive error if orphaned records exist, preventing a silent FK violation during constraint creation.

changeSet.author: travel-insurance
changeSet.comment:
Adding UNIQUE constraint on medical_risk_limit_levels.code.
WHY: PostgreSQL requires the referenced column to have a UNIQUE or PRIMARY KEY constraint for FK creation. The existing UK is composite (code, valid_from), which does not satisfy this requirement for a single-column FK reference.
SEMANTICS: medical level codes (e.g. '10000', '50000') are globally unique identifiers — versioning via valid_from does not introduce duplicate codes. This constraint enforces that invariant explicitly.

rollback.sql: ALTER TABLE medical_risk_limit_levels DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS uk_medical_level_code_unique;

changeSet.author: travel-insurance
changeSet.comment:
Adding UNIQUE constraint on countries.iso_code.
WHY: Same reason as 017-02 — FK requires a single-column UNIQUE constraint.
SEMANTICS: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country codes are globally unique by definition. The composite UK (iso_code, valid_from) tracks rate versioning, not identity. This constraint makes the uniqueness of codes explicit and FK-compatible.

rollback.sql: ALTER TABLE countries DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS uk_country_iso_code_unique;

changeSet.author: travel-insurance
changeSet.comment:
Adding FK constraint: agreements.medical_risk_limit_level → medical_risk_limit_levels.code.
Prevents creating agreements with non-existent medical coverage levels. Data quality pre-checked in changeSet 017-01.
changeSet.sql: ALTER TABLE agreements ADD CONSTRAINT fk_agreement_medical_level FOREIGN KEY (medical_risk_limit_level) REFERENCES medical_risk_limit_levels(code);

rollback.sql: ALTER TABLE agreements DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS fk_agreement_medical_level;

changeSet.author: travel-insurance
changeSet.comment:
Adding FK constraint: agreements.country_iso_code → countries.iso_code.
Prevents creating agreements with non-existent country codes. Data quality pre-checked in changeSet 017-01.
changeSet.sql: ALTER TABLE agreements ADD CONSTRAINT fk_agreement_country FOREIGN KEY (country_iso_code) REFERENCES countries(iso_code);

rollback.sql: ALTER TABLE agreements DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS fk_agreement_country;

changeSet.author: travel-insurance
changeSet.comment: Adding documentation comments to new constraints and columns
changeSet.sql: COMMENT ON CONSTRAINT fk_agreement_medical_level ON agreements IS 'FK added in 017 (п. 2.3 refactoring plan). Ensures agreements.medical_risk_limit_level references a valid code in medical_risk_limit_levels. Previously absent, allowing orphaned data.';

COMMENT ON CONSTRAINT fk_agreement_country ON agreements IS 'FK added in 017 (п. 2.3 refactoring plan). Ensures agreements.country_iso_code references a valid ISO code in countries. Previously absent, allowing orphaned data.';
